note: Generated by scripts/gen-schema-docs.ts. Do not edit by hand.

fields.name: gateway
fields.description: Gateway-wide settings (auth, SOTW, pulse, heartbeat, SSRF, logging).

fields.name: mcp_servers
fields.description: Upstream MCP servers this gateway proxies.

sections.title: gateway
sections.description: Gateway-wide settings (auth, SOTW, pulse, heartbeat, SSRF, logging).

fields.name: authentication
fields.description: Gateway authentication settings.

fields.name: advanced
fields.description: Arbitrary KEY=VALUE lines appended verbatim to the deployed env file. Last-occurrence-wins under systemd EnvironmentFile semantics, so entries here override template-rendered values for the same key.
fields.rationale: Escape hatch for env vars not covered by a typed schema field above. Use only when no typed field fits — any key this schema already emits is rejected at validation time, so you cannot accidentally shadow a typed field.

fields.name: sotw
fields.description: State-of-the-world reconciliation overrides.

fields.name: pulse
fields.description: Pulse reporter overrides.

fields.name: heartbeat
fields.description: Heartbeat overrides.

fields.name: ssrf
fields.description: SSRF protection overrides. Strict defaults apply when omitted.

fields.name: log_level
fields.description: ContextForge log level.
fields.rationale: Raise to `DEBUG` or `TRACE` to diagnose a specific issue; lower to `WARNING` or `ERROR` to reduce log volume in production.

fields.name: allowed_origins
fields.description: CORS allowlist; JSON-encoded array in the env file.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: When omitted, defaults to true and authentication is enabled. Sets MCP_REQUIRE_AUTH accordingly.
fields.rationale: Leave at the default `true` for production. Set `false` only for local development where you want an unauthenticated gateway.

fields.name: jwks_info
fields.description: JWT validation settings. Required when the gateway validates bearer tokens directly.

fields.name: sso_issuer
fields.description: SSO issuer URL for generic SSO discovery.
fields.rationale: Set when you're using an SSO provider that publishes an OpenID Connect discovery document at `{issuer}/.well-known/openid-configuration`.

fields.name: jwt_issuer_verification
fields.description: Whether to verify the JWT iss claim.

fields.name: jwt_audience_verification
fields.description: Whether to verify the JWT aud claim.

fields.name: require_jti
fields.description: Require a jti claim on each token.

fields.name: require_token_expiration
fields.description: Require an exp claim on each token.

fields.name: mcp_oauth_resource_metadata_enabled
fields.description: Enable RFC 9728 OAuth resource metadata discovery.

fields.name: sso_generic_scope
fields.description: OAuth scope string for the generic SSO discovery flow.
fields.rationale: Set to the OAuth scope your SSO provider requires (e.g. `openid profile email`). Ignored when `sso_issuer` is not set.

fields.name: oauth_dcr
fields.description: OAuth Dynamic Client Registration overrides. Defaults are auto-derived from mcp_servers; set fields here to override (typically to disable DCR/discovery for IdPs that pre-provision clients).

fields.name: well_known_allow_http
fields.description: When true, forces the gateway to grant tokens to http URLs. This is used to run the container in local mode.

crossFieldConstraints.message: When authentication is enabled (the default), "jwks_info" must be configured. "sso_issuer" alone is metadata-only and does not validate tokens.

sections.title: gateway.authentication.jwks_info
sections.description: JWT validation settings. Required when the gateway validates bearer tokens directly.

fields.name: jwt_algorithm
fields.description: Signing algorithm for bearer tokens.
fields.rationale: Choose the algorithm your identity provider uses to sign tokens — HS256 for symmetric secrets, RS256 for asymmetric JWKs.

fields.name: jwt_jwks_uri
fields.description: JWKS discovery endpoint for public keys.
fields.rationale: Set to the JWKS URL your identity provider publishes; the gateway fetches public keys from here to validate incoming tokens.

fields.name: jwt_issuer
fields.description: Expected iss claim value.
fields.rationale: Set to the `iss` claim your provider emits; the gateway rejects tokens whose `iss` does not match.

fields.name: jwt_audience
fields.description: Expected aud claim value.
fields.rationale: Set to the `aud` claim the provider targets at this gateway; prevents tokens meant for other services from being accepted.

sections.title: gateway.authentication.oauth_dcr
sections.description: OAuth Dynamic Client Registration overrides. Defaults are auto-derived from mcp_servers; set fields here to override (typically to disable DCR/discovery for IdPs that pre-provision clients).

fields.name: dcr_enabled
fields.description: Enable Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591) against upstream OAuth issuers.
fields.rationale: Set `false` when your IdP (e.g. Entra ID) does not support DCR and OAuth clients are pre-provisioned out of band; leave unset to inherit the default driven by `mcp_servers[*].authentication.issuer`.

fields.name: auto_register_on_missing_credentials
fields.description: Automatically register a client with the issuer when client_id/client_secret are absent.
fields.rationale: Set `false` to require pre-provisioned client credentials and fail closed when they are missing.

fields.name: oauth_discovery_enabled
fields.description: Enable RFC 8414 OAuth authorization-server metadata discovery on the gateway.
fields.rationale: Set `false` when the upstream IdP does not publish authorization-server metadata (common with config-only Entra deployments).

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the SOTW reconciliation loop runs.

fields.name: file_path
fields.description: Path to the SOTW YAML file on the gateway host.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the pulse reporter to d2 runs.

fields.name: interval_seconds
fields.description: Interval between pulses in seconds.

fields.name: event_driven_enabled
fields.description: Whether event-driven pulse emission is enabled.

fields.name: debounce_seconds
fields.description: Debounce window for event-driven pulses in seconds.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Whether the gateway-side heartbeat runs.

fields.name: interval_seconds
fields.description: Interval between heartbeats in seconds.

sections.title: gateway.ssrf
sections.description: SSRF protection overrides. Strict defaults apply when omitted.

fields.name: allow_localhost
fields.description: Allow outbound connections to localhost / 127.0.0.1.
fields.rationale: Enable only for local development where the MCP server runs on the same host as the gateway; leaving it on in production widens the gateway's outbound attack surface.

fields.name: allow_private_networks
fields.description: Allow outbound connections to RFC 1918 private networks.
fields.rationale: Enable only when the gateway and MCP server share a private network (e.g. co-located on one EC2 host); prefer `allowed_networks` with a surgical CIDR allowlist in production, since a blanket private-range allow widens the gateway's outbound attack surface.

fields.name: allowed_networks
fields.description: CIDR allowlist for outbound connections; JSON-encoded in the env file.
fields.rationale: Set to the specific CIDRs your MCP servers live on when the gateway must reach private hosts — prefer this surgical allowlist over the blanket `allow_private_networks=true`, since every range you add widens the gateway's outbound attack surface.

fields.name: dns_fail_closed
fields.description: Block the request if DNS resolution fails.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Server display name.
fields.rationale: A short memorable identifier shown in the d2 UI.

fields.name: description
fields.description: Free-text description shown in the UI.
fields.rationale: One-line summary so end users know what this server offers.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Upstream MCP server endpoint URL.
fields.rationale: Where the gateway forwards MCP requests for this server.

fields.name: transport_type
fields.description: MCP transport protocol. Uppercased and underscore-stripped at emission.
fields.rationale: `http` for most servers; `sse` only when the server explicitly requires server-sent events.

fields.name: authentication
fields.description: Authentication strategy used when the gateway connects to this server.

fields.name: refresh_interval_seconds
fields.description: Gateway refresh interval for this server.
fields.rationale: How often the gateway re-fetches the server's tool list. Raise when the list changes rarely, lower when it changes often.

fields.name: visibility
fields.description: Who can discover this server in the UI.
fields.rationale: `public` lists the server to everyone; `team` lists to your tenant only; `private` hides it from discovery.

fields.name: owner_email
fields.description: Contact email for the server owner.
fields.rationale: Who to contact when this server breaks.

sections.title: mcp_servers[].authentication
sections.description:
Authentication strategy used when the gateway connects to this server.
Discriminated union keyed on `type`. Variants:
- `bearer` — see [`mcp_servers[].authentication (bearer)`](#mcp_serversauthentication-bearer)
- `basic` — see [`mcp_servers[].authentication (basic)`](#mcp_serversauthentication-basic)
- `authheaders` — see [`mcp_servers[].authentication (authheaders)`](#mcp_serversauthentication-authheaders)
- `query_param` — see [`mcp_servers[].authentication (query_param)`](#mcp_serversauthentication-query_param)
- `oauth` — see [`mcp_servers[].authentication (oauth)`](#mcp_serversauthentication-oauth)
- `cert` — see [`mcp_servers[].authentication (cert)`](#mcp_serversauthentication-cert)

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator for bearer-token auth.

fields.name: token
fields.description: Bearer token sent in the Authorization header.
fields.rationale: Paste the static bearer token the upstream server issued you.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator for HTTP basic auth.

fields.name: username
fields.description: Basic-auth username.
fields.rationale: Account name for the upstream server when it uses HTTP basic auth.

fields.name: password
fields.description: Basic-auth password.
fields.rationale: Account password for HTTP basic auth; stored encrypted at rest.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator for custom auth-header auth.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: One or more header name/value pairs.
fields.rationale: Use when the server requires a custom header name (e.g. `X-Api-Key`) instead of `Authorization`.

fields.name: key
fields.description: Header name.
fields.rationale: The header name the server expects (e.g. `X-Api-Key`).

fields.name: value
fields.description: Header value.
fields.rationale: The token or key sent in the header.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator for query-parameter auth.

fields.name: param_key
fields.description: Query parameter name.
fields.rationale: The URL query parameter name the server reads for auth (e.g. `api_key`).

fields.name: param_value
fields.description: Query parameter value.
fields.rationale: The key or token sent in the query parameter.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator for OAuth 2.0 auth.

fields.name: grant_type
fields.description: OAuth grant type.
fields.rationale: Pick the OAuth grant the upstream server supports — `client_credentials` for machine-to-machine, `authorization_code` for delegated user auth.

fields.name: client_id
fields.description: OAuth client ID.
fields.rationale: The OAuth client identifier the upstream server issued you. Omit to let the gateway register dynamically (requires `issuer`).

fields.name: client_secret
fields.description: OAuth client secret.
fields.rationale: The OAuth client secret paired with `client_id`. Omit for public clients or when using DCR.

fields.name: token_url
fields.description: OAuth token endpoint.
fields.rationale: The token endpoint the gateway posts to. Omit when `issuer` is set — DCR will discover it.

fields.name: authorization_url
fields.description: OAuth authorization endpoint.
fields.rationale: The authorize endpoint for delegated user flows. Omit for non-interactive grants like `client_credentials`.

fields.name: redirect_uri
fields.description: OAuth redirect URI.
fields.rationale: Callback URL the upstream server redirects back to after user consent.

fields.name: issuer
fields.description: OAuth issuer URL; enables DCR when set without client_id/secret/token_url.
fields.rationale: Set to enable dynamic client registration — the gateway discovers token/authorize URLs and registers itself automatically.

fields.name: scopes
fields.description: OAuth scopes requested.
fields.rationale: Scopes the gateway requests from the provider; match the provider's documented scope strings.

fields.name: pkce_enabled
fields.description: Enable PKCE on the authorization flow.
fields.rationale: Enable for public clients where leaking the `client_secret` is a risk.

fields.name: oauth_quirks
fields.description: Provider-specific compatibility flags.

fields.name: extra_authorize_params
fields.description: Extra query parameters appended to the authorize URL (keys preserve case).

fields.name: token_response_path
fields.description: Dotted path into the token response for nested access tokens.

fields.name: scope_param_name
fields.description: Override the OAuth scope parameter name.

fields.name: scope_separator
fields.description: Separator used when joining scopes.

fields.name: token_lifetime_seconds
fields.description: Override for how long access tokens are cached.

crossFieldConstraints.message: OAuth requires either "issuer" or all of "client_id", "client_secret", and "token_url"

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator for client-certificate auth.

fields.name: ca_cert
fields.description: PEM-encoded CA certificate.
fields.rationale: Paste the PEM-encoded CA cert when the upstream server uses a custom CA (mTLS or self-signed).

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator for no gateway-managed auth.
